question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: hapiVersion()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a record stream file with hapiVersionMajor=0, hapiVersionMinor=0, hapiVersionPatch=0 to force the system to return HAPI_VERSION_NOT_SET, causing downstream fee-schedule selection logic to fall back to legacy (lower) fee parameters and undercharge transaction fees?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: hapiVersion()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a malformed record file where hapiVersionMajor, hapiVersionMinor, or hapiVersionPatch is null, triggering the HAPI_VERSION_NOT_SET fallback (Version(0,0,0)) and causing fee-gating version checks (e.g., isGreaterThanOrEqualTo(HAPI_VERSION_0_47_0)) to evaluate false, bypassing higher fee schedules?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: hapiVersion()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a record file with hapiVersionMajor/Minor/Patch set to values below HAPI_VERSION_0_23_0 so that version-gated fee logic treats the file as pre-0.23.0, applying an older fee schedule and reducing charged_tx_fee for all items in the file?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: hapiVersion()] Since hapiVersion is a @Getter(lazy=true) @Transient field computed once from hapiVersionMajor/Minor/Patch, can an unprivileged external user exploit a race condition where hapiVersionMajor is set to a low value before the lazy field is initialized, locking in a stale low-version value that causes fee-schedule lookups to use outdated (cheaper) fee parameters for the entire file's lifetime?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: hapiVersion()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a record file where hapiVersionMajor/Minor/Patch are set to values just below HAPI_VERSION_0_27_0 (e.g., 0.26.x), causing the importer to skip fee-schedule behaviors introduced in 0.27.0 and record artificially low charged_tx_fee values in the database?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: hapiVersion()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a record file with hapiVersionMajor/Minor/Patch just below HAPI_VERSION_0_47_0 to bypass fee-related behavioral changes introduced at that version boundary, causing the mirror node to store and serve incorrect (lower) fee data for transactions in that file?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: hapiVersion()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a record file with hapiVersionMajor/Minor/Patch just below HAPI_VERSION_0_49_0 to prevent fee-schedule behaviors introduced at 0.49.0 from applying, causing the mirror node to report stale or lower fee estimates to downstream consumers?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: hapiVersion()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a record file with hapiVersionMajor/Minor/Patch just below HAPI_VERSION_0_53_0 to bypass the most recent fee-schedule changes, causing the mirror node to serve fee estimates that are lower than the current network fee schedule?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: RecordFile (builder)] Since @Data generates public setters for all fields including hapiVersionMajor, hapiVersionMinor, and hapiVersionPatch, can an unprivileged external user exploit a deserialization or API endpoint that accepts a RecordFile JSON payload to overwrite these version fields post-construction, causing the lazy hapiVersion to be stale and fee-schedule selection to use the wrong version?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: copy()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the copy() method (via toBuilder().build()) on a RecordFile with manipulated hapiVersionMajor/Minor/Patch values, producing a cloned RecordFile that propagates incorrect version-based fee parameters into downstream processing pipelines?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: copy()] Can an unprivileged external user cause copy() to produce a RecordFile where the lazy hapiVersion field is re-evaluated from manipulated hapiVersionMajor/Minor/Patch values, resulting in a different (lower) version being used for fee calculations in the copied instance compared to the original?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: clear()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger clear() on a RecordFile to null out logsBloom and reset sidecars while leaving gasUsed intact at an attacker-controlled value, causing block_gas_used in API responses to reflect an inflated or deflated gas figure that distorts EVM fee calculations?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: clear()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that clear() resets sidecars to List.of() but does not reset gasUsed, causing a RecordFile to report a non-zero gasUsed with no corresponding sidecar records, leading to inflated block_gas_used values that affect EVM transaction fee reporting?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: isEmpty()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the reference-equality check in isEmpty() (this == EMPTY) by constructing a RecordFile with all fields matching EMPTY but as a distinct object, causing isEmpty() to return false and the file to be processed as a valid fee-bearing record file when it should be skipped?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: isEmpty()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a record file that passes isEmpty() == false (since it is not the EMPTY singleton) but contains zero-fee or negative-fee RecordItems in its items list, causing the importer to persist fraudulent zero-fee transactions to the database?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: RecordFile (field: gasUsed)] Since gasUsed has @Builder.Default value of 0L and is a mutable long field, can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted record file where gasUsed is set to Long.MAX_VALUE, causing integer overflow in downstream gas-to-fee conversion logic and producing incorrect (near-zero or negative) fee values in API responses?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: RecordFile (field: gasUsed)] Can an unprivileged external user inject a record file with gasUsed=0 despite containing EVM contract call transactions, causing block_gas_used to be reported as 0 in the REST API and misleading fee estimation consumers into believing no gas was consumed?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: RecordFile (field: gasUsed)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a record file with a negative gasUsed value (since it is a primitive long with no lower-bound validation), causing downstream fee calculations that rely on gasUsed to produce negative or zero fee estimates?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: RecordFile (field: consensusEnd)] Since consensusEnd is the @Id (primary key) and is a nullable Long, can an unprivileged external user supply a record file with a consensusEnd value that falls within an existing fee-schedule validity window, causing the importer to associate transactions with an incorrect (older, cheaper) fee schedule?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: RecordFile (field: consensusStart)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a record file where consensusStart is set to a timestamp that predates the current fee schedule's effective time, causing fee-schedule lookups keyed on consensusStart to retrieve an older, lower-cost fee schedule and underreport charged_tx_fee?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: RecordFile (field: consensusStart/consensusEnd)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a record file where consensusStart > consensusEnd, violating the temporal ordering invariant and causing fee-schedule range queries (which use consensusEnd >= timestamp) to return no results or incorrect fee schedules?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: RecordFile (field: version)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a record file with version set to an unexpected integer (e.g., 1 or 99), causing the RecordFileReader to select a different parsing path that does not validate fee fields, allowing arbitrary charged_tx_fee values to be ingested?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: RecordFile (field: version)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a record file with version=2 (pre-V5 format) to trigger AbstractPreV5RecordFileReader parsing, which reads transaction fees directly from raw bytes without the same validation as the ProtoRecordFileReader, allowing manipulated fee values to be stored?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: RecordFile (field: previousHash)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a record file with a forged previousHash that matches the hash of a legitimate file, breaking the hash chain integrity check and allowing a file with manipulated fee data to be accepted as a valid continuation of the stream?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: RecordFile (field: hash)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a record file where the hash field is set to a value that matches a previously accepted file's hash, causing the importer to treat a fee-manipulated file as a duplicate of a legitimate file and skip integrity validation?
